title: 10 best herbal essential oils for premenstrual syndrome
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for premenstrual syndrome are plant-based extracts that help alleviate symptoms of PMS, a condition characterized by mood swings, cramps, and breast tenderness. These oils, including Angelica archangelica, Zingiber officinale, Melissa officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Valeriana officinalis, Paeonia lactiflora, Vitex agnus-castus, Cimicifuga racemosa, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Ginkgo biloba, have anti-inflammatory and mood-regulating properties that reduce PMS symptoms. Without effective treatment, PMS can disrupt daily life, causing anxiety, fatigue, and irritability, negatively impacting relationships, work, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its calming and balancing properties. The oil's antioxidant-rich composition helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, anxiety, and breast tenderness. Its ability to regulate hormonal fluctuations also helps to ease cramps and hot flashes, providing relief from the discomforts associated with PMS. Furthermore, sage oil's relaxing scent can promote a sense of calmness and well-being, helping to reduce stress and improve overall mental health during this time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to PMS symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate symptoms such as cramps, headaches, and fatigue by reducing prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation in the body.

Uterine-toning properties: Sage essential oil's uterine-toning properties help regulate menstrual cycles, reduce heavy bleeding, and ease dysmenorrhea (painful periods) by toning the uterus and improving its contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of PMS such as cramps and mood swings.
Barks: Sage bark is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and reduce bloating, common complaints during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia sclarea leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated weighing 100 grams.
Clean and chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area exposing all parts of plant.
Combine chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a dark glass bottle and allow it to infuse for 14 days shaking the bottle daily to facilitate extraction process.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids and storing extracted oil in refrigerator at 20 degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because it possesses a calming and soothing effect on the body. Lavender oil has been shown to reduce stress, anxiety, and mood swings, which are common symptoms of PMS. Its gentle and peaceful aroma can also help alleviate physical discomforts such as cramps, bloating, and fatigue. Additionally, lavender oil's ability to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality can be particularly beneficial during the premenstrual phase when many women experience insomnia and restlessness.
remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative Effects: Lavender oil's calming and sedative effects can help reduce anxiety, stress, and mood swings associated with PMS.
Pain Relief: The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender oil may help alleviate cramps, headaches, and breast tenderness that occur during the premenstrual phase.

Hormone Regulation: Lavender oil's ability to regulate hormonal balance may help reduce symptoms such as bloating, water retention, and mood swings by supporting the body's natural hormone production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating and water retention associated with PMS.
Flowers: Contain linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce anxiety and mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh lavender flowers and foliage from a reputable source for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested lavender material in a warm dark place or using a dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried lavender material using a glass still and water ratio of 1:5 for 4-6 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distilled condensate and impurities using a separating funnel and clean cotton cloth.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining impurities before storage in dark glass containers at room temperature for 6-12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has a calming and soothing effect on the body. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce breast tenderness, mood swings, and bloating that are common symptoms of PMS. Additionally, chamomile's gentle sedative properties promote relaxation, alleviating anxiety, insomnia, and cramps associated with PMS. By incorporating chamomile essential oils into one's self-care routine, individuals can experience a reduction in symptoms and improved overall well-being during their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings associated with PMS by reducing inflammation in the body.

Anxiolytic property: The anxiolytic properties of chamomile essential oil help calm anxiety, stress, and irritability often experienced during PMS, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms like mood swings and cramps.

Antispasmodic property: Chamomile essential oil's antispasmodic properties help ease menstrual cramps, back pain, and other physical discomforts associated with PMS by relaxing muscle spasms and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots contain soothing compounds that help alleviate symptoms of PMS, such as anxiety and restlessness.
Flowers: The daisy-like flowers of the chamomile plant are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that can help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them according to traditional methods within two days of harvesting.
Combine one part dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with four parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed in a glass jar.
Place the mixture in a warm dark place for 14 days to allow for solvent extraction and gentle maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids after two hours of straining time.
Transfer the extracted oil to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its natural ability to balance hormones, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate physical discomforts. The oil's sweet, floral scent has a calming effect on the nervous system, soothing irritability and mood swings that often accompany PMS. Additionally, geranium oil's anti-inflammatory properties help ease breast tenderness and abdominal cramps, while its hormone-regulating effects may even help reduce menstrual cramping and heavy bleeding.
remedy_properties: Uterine Tonic Properties: Geranium oil's uterine tonic properties can help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with PMS, reducing symptoms like breast tenderness and mood swings.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in geranium essential oil can alleviate physical symptoms of PMS such as cramps, bloating, and fatigue by reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Mood-Stabilizing Properties: Geranium oil's ability to balance the body's emotional response can help reduce anxiety, irritability, and mood swings associated with PMS, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Geranium roots contain compounds that help regulate hormonal balance, reducing symptoms of PMS such as mood swings and bloating.
Leaves: Geranium leaves possess anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate cramps, headaches, and breast tenderness associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves at dawn when the dew is still present and store them in a paper bag.
Chop 15 grams of harvested leaves into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife for better diffusion.
Steam distill chopped leaves with 250 milliliters of distilled water in an apparatus for 2 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has a profound impact on relieving symptoms associated with PMS. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe cramps and discomfort, while its antispasmodic effects calm anxiety and tension. Additionally, yarrow's estrogen-regulating qualities can help alleviate breast tenderness and mood swings. Its calming and balancing properties also promote relaxation, reducing irritability and emotional sensitivity, allowing women to feel more centered and comfortable during the premenstrual phase of their cycle.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Yarrow essential oil helps to relax uterine muscles and reduce cramps associated with PMS, providing relief from painful periods.
Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the abdomen, breasts, and other areas affected by PMS.
Antioxidant property: Yarrow essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to mood swings, fatigue, and other PMS symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve menstrual cramps associated with PMS.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression often experienced during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 3-4 hours or air dry for 7-10 days.
Use a steam distillation apparatus with a capacity of 1 liter to extract the essential oil from 100 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Monitor the distillation process for 2-3 hours, collecting the extracted essential oil in a glass container as it is produced.
Filter the collected essential oil using a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove any impurities or sediment, resulting in a clear golden liquid with a rich, earthy aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because it provides a natural relief from physical symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness. The menthol content in peppermint oil has a warming effect on the muscles, helping to ease cramps and spasms, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's calming aroma can also help alleviate emotional symptoms like anxiety and mood swings, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being during this time of the month.
remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Peppermint oil's ability to ease digestive discomfort, such as bloating and cramps, helps reduce PMS symptoms like constipation and nausea.
Antispasmodic Properties: Peppermint oil's antispasmodic properties help relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can alleviate menstrual cramps, headaches, and breast tenderness associated with PMS.

Anti-Anxiety and Stress-Reducing Effects: The calming scent of peppermint oil has a grounding effect on the mind and body, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to PMS symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and soothing oil that helps alleviate cramps, headaches, and irritability associated with premenstrual syndrome.
Leaves: The leaves contain menthol, which is effective in relieving bloating, cramps, and breast tenderness caused by hormonal fluctuations during the menstrual cycle.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves at dawn in full bloom and dry them properly for optimal potency.
Combine 250 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding solids and retaining the liquid.
Transfer the resulting essential oil mixture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because its anise-like scent can alleviate symptoms of bloating, cramps, and mood swings. The oil's natural properties help to ease digestive discomfort by reducing inflammation in the stomach and uterus, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. Additionally, fennel's estrogen-balancing effects can help regulate hormonal fluctuations, leading to a decrease in PMS-related symptoms such as anxiety, irritability, and breast tenderness.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel essential oil has been shown to reduce bloating, cramps, and digestive discomfort associated with PMS due to its ability to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of fennel essential oil help to ease muscle cramps, spasms, and tension often experienced during the premenstrual phase, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Hormone-balancing properties: Fennel essential oil has been found to have a positive effect on hormone levels, particularly estrogen and progesterone, which can help alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and fatigue commonly associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have a calming effect, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with PMS.
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain compounds that may help ease cramps and reduce inflammation, making them useful in relieving menstrual discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried fennel seeds in quantities of 100 grams per batch.
Steam distill the fennel seeds using a clevenger apparatus for 4-6 hours.
Filter the distilled oil through paper coffee filters into dark glass bottles.
Store the essential oils at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.
Use the foeniculum vulgare essential oils topically diluted with carrier oil in a 1:10 ratio.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain compounds that have a profound impact on hormonal balance, easing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings. Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dill essential oil, such as carvotanacetone and dillapiole, may help alleviate symptoms like cramps, back pain, and headaches associated with PMS by reducing inflammation in the body.

Flowers: Dill flowers are a rich source of esters and terpenes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, making them effective in reducing menstrual pain and anxiety.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of anethum graveolens in the early morning for optimal oil quality.
Dry the leaves using air circulation or desiccant method for at least 7 days.
Blend dried leaves with carrier oil in a ratio of 1:5 for proper dilution and infusion.
Steam distill the blend using a clevenger apparatus for 2 hours to extract essential oils.
Filter and decant the extracted essential oil into dark glass bottles for storage and usage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain limonene, a natural chemical that has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels. The citrusy scent of bitter orange oil also has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate symptoms such as mood swings and irritability associated with PMS. Uterotonic Property: The uterotonic property of bitter orange essential oil can help to regulate menstruation and alleviate cramps, bloating, and other physical symptoms related to PMS.

Antioxidant Property: As an antioxidant, bitter orange essential oil helps to neutralize free radicals in the body, which may contribute to the alleviation of symptoms such as mood swings, irritability, and depression that often accompany PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, bitter orange roots are valued for their calming and soothing properties, making them effective in relieving symptoms of premenstrual syndrome such as anxiety and cramps.
Barks: The barks of the bitter orange plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramps, making them a popular choice for medicinal essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest citrus x aurantium peels from mature trees in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested peels using shade or sun drying for 7-10 days, turning occasionally.
Steam distill the dried peels in a stainless steel alembic at a temperature of 100°c for 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal and decant into dark glass containers.
Store the essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense essential oils helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with PMS. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Frankincense essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acid, which can help alleviate PMS symptoms like breast tenderness and cramping by reducing inflammation in the body.

Antioxidant property: Frankincense essential oil is rich in antioxidants, which can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to PMS symptoms like mood swings, anxiety, and depression, promoting overall well-being.
Anxiolytic property: The anxiolytic compounds present in frankincense essential oil, such as α-pinene, can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress associated with PMS.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia serrata gum resin from reputable sources ensuring optimal freshness.
Clean and dry the harvested gum resin thoroughly for at least 24 hours before processing.
Steam distill the dried gum resin using a steam distillation apparatus at 100-150°c for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through activated charcoal or cotton wool to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final boswellia serrata essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to one year.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat premenstrual syndrome with herbal essential oils is by using a blend of oils that address symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and mood swings. Clary sage oil has anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate cramps, while peppermint oil helps soothe digestive issues. Geranium oil regulates hormonal imbalances and reduces anxiety. Blend these oils with a carrier oil and apply topically or diffuse them in the air for effective relief from PMS symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to premenstrual syndrome that are treated with herbal essential oils are menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and mood swings. Herbal essential oils like Clary Sage, Lavender, and Geranium have been used to alleviate symptoms of perimenopause and menopause. Additionally, essential oils like Bergamot, Ylang-Ylang, and Jasmine are used to treat anxiety and depression, which can be exacerbated by hormonal fluctuations during these life stages.
